channel.title: Api on Sam Harrison
channel.description: Recent content in Api on Sam Harrison

item.title: Retrieving water quality data from the Environment Agency's new API
item.description:
The Environment Agency has a treasure trove of data on water quality in English rivers, made openly available via a web portal, with an accompanying API.

Back at the end of last year (2025) the EA refreshed the web portal, removing a handy option to bulk download data. At the same time, they committed the cardinal sin of completely changing their API, deprecating their old API at the same time, with very little notice. Those of us that used the old API were left with broken scripts and fair barrier to accessing the data, compounding by rather opaque documentation.

item.title: Getting more out of your Tado heating system with Python and the Tado API
item.description: We have recently installed a Tado thermostat to control our heating system. Tado is a “smart” heating system, in the sense that it is internet connected and lets you control your heating via its own app. The app is surprisingly good, but lacking in a few places, and so this got me wondering whether Tado had an API, and whether I could leverage this to get a bit more out of our heating system. The two specific things I wanted to achieve were:
